description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the node:latest Docker image, defining stages build, test, and deploy, and setting DS_DEFAULT_ANALYZERS=retire.js and SAST_DEFAULT_ANALYZERS=eslint variables. In the build stage add a build_package job that caches node_modules/, runs npm install --unsafe-perm -g vsce then npm install --unsafe-perm, packages the extension with vsce package -o $CI_PROJECT_NAME-$CI_JOB_ID-$CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE.vsix --baseContentUrl $CI_PROJECT_URL/blob/$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME, stores *.vsix artifacts named artifacts-$CI_PROJECT_NAME-$CI_JOB_ID for 2 weeks, and runs only on the develop branch, merge request events, or any tag except tags matching ^v.*$ (which are excluded). Also in the build stage add a build_package_release job that caches node_modules/, runs the same npm commands, packages with vsce package -o $CI_PROJECT_NAME-$CI_COMMIT_TAG.vsix --baseContentUrl $BASE_CONTENT_URL, publishes *.vsix artifacts named artifacts-$CI_PROJECT_NAME-$CI_COMMIT_TAG, and runs only on tags matching ^v.*$. In the test stage include code_quality, eslint-sast, license_scanning, retire-js-dependency_scanning, and secret_detection jobs (provided by the included SAST, License-Scanning, Secret-Detection, Dependency-Scanning, and Code-Quality templates) all triggered on tags, the develop branch, merge request events, and for license and dependency scans also on scheduled pipelines (license_scanning sets NODE_ENV=production). In the deploy stage add a deploy_package job that uses node:latest, sets GIT_STRATEGY=none, runs npm install --unsafe-perm -g vsce then vsce publish -p $VSCE_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN --packagePath $CI_PROJECT_NAME-$CI_COMMIT_TAG.vsix, and runs only on tags matching ^v.*$.
